title: quercus brantii subsp. oophora
intro: Quercus brantii subsp. oophora, also known as the Persian oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to the mountainous regions of Iran and Turkey. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus brantii subsp. oophora include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
